title: cerinthe minor proles auriculata
intro: Cerinthe minor proles auriculata, also known as Honeywort, is a perennial plant species in the borage family that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Honeywort has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Honeywort include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Honeywort can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal and health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
